methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $529M - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $6B+

private.name: Alto Neuroscience

data.narrative:
Recursion Pharmaceuticals is a public-stage biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector, currently valued at $1.8B after raising over $1B in total capital. The peer universe comprises 3 public comparables (Tempus AI, Freenome, Alto Neuroscience) and 8 private frontier-tech peers across the broader life-sciences ecosystem.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable valuation approach given data constraints. Across the 3 same-sector, same-stage public comparables, the median valuation is $2.6B, with a range spanning $529M (Alto Neuroscience) to $6.0B (Tempus AI). This method anchors the valuation directly to observable market multiples for functionally similar-stage businesses. The public-comp anchoring method using sector price-to-sales multiples (7.8x median across 3 biotech tickers) is not applicable, as the target's revenue is not disclosed—a common constraint in early-stage private and public biotech valuations.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $2.2B to $3.0B, with a median of $2.6B. This implies the current $1.8B valuation sits below peer medians by approximately 31%. Key uncertainties include the target's cash position, pipeline advancement relative to named comparables, and whether recent public-market volatility in biotech has compressed peer multiples since comparables were last marked.
